records.ety: [Pref. be- + troth, i. e., truth. See Truth.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Betrothed (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Betrothing.]

defs: To contract to any one for a marriage; to engage or promise in order to marriage; to affiance; -- used esp. of a woman.
quotes: He, in the first flower of my freshest age, Betrothed me unto the only heir.

defs: To promise to take (as a future spouse); to plight one's troth to.
quotes: What man is there that hath betrothed a wife, and hath not taken her?

defs: To nominate to a bishopric, in order to consecration.
